Details: Inactivation of B. anthracis spores in water by chlorine can be very effective (2- to 4-log inactivation) where the effectiveness is dependent on pH, chlorine residual, CT or contact time, and temperature. pH has the most significant impact on efficacy of chlorine for B. anthracis spore inactivation. When the pH is 10 or higher and the temperature is less than ambient, the contact time or the chlorine concentration must be increased substantially to achieve a 2-log inactivation.
